docs.title: The Review Flywheel
docs.text:
How Climbo compounds value over time. Each piece connects to the next.

Customer gets SMS/email
        ↓
Taps link → leaves Google review
        ↓
SEO Agent writes keyword-rich reply  →  ranks higher on Maps
        ↓
Social Agent posts it to GBP  →  more activity signal
        ↓
GEO Agent publishes it as content  →  appears in ChatGPT / AI Overviews
        ↓
More visibility → more walk-ins → more customers → more reviews

:octicons-arrow-right-24: See how the AI agents work

docs.title: About this wiki
docs.text:
Built from 30 YouTube transcripts (Jan–Jul 2026) and 933 Skool community posts. Last full update: July 11, 2026.

This is a community resource. If you find something outdated or missing, open an issue on GitHub.

Maintainer note: This wiki follows the LLM Wiki pattern — an LLM builds and maintains the pages; humans source and direct. To add a new source (AMA, post, feature update), drop it in the repo and the wiki updates.

docs.title: Climbo LLM Wiki — Schema
docs.text: This document is the operating manual for the LLM that maintains this wiki. Read it fully before touching any wiki file.

docs.title: What this wiki is
docs.text:
A persistent, interlinked knowledge base about Climbo — the white-label AI review and local SEO platform. Built from: - 30 YouTube transcripts from @climboapp (Jan–Jul 2026) - 933 Skool community posts (Nov 2025–Jul 2026) - Public site and authenticated app documentation

Audience: New Climbo agency owners who just signed up and don't know where to start. Tone: clear, direct, no fluff, no jargon unless explained. English.

You (the LLM) own this layer entirely. The human reads it; you write and maintain it.

docs.title: Page format
docs.text:
Every wiki page follows this template:

# Page Title

> **One-line summary** of what this page covers.

<!-- Optional: frontmatter block -->
---
tags: [feature, review-requests]
sources: [BRocA7husPU, skool-post-XXXX]
last_updated: 2026-07-11
---

## Overview
[2-3 paragraphs max. What is this, why does it matter, when do you use it]

## How it works
[Step by step if procedural, or structured explanation if conceptual]

## Key details / Tips
[Bullet points of gotchas, best practices, common mistakes]

## Related
- [[other-page]] — why it's related
- [[another-page]] — why it's related

## Sources
- YouTube: [Video Title](videoId) — timestamp if relevant
- Skool: post slug or topic

Rules: - [[wiki-link]] syntax for internal links (Obsidian-compatible) - Never exceed 500 words per page unless the topic requires it - No walls of text — use headers, bullets, short paragraphs - Every page must have at least one ## Related entry - Every page must list its ## Sources

docs.title: Ingest workflow
docs.text:
When adding a new source: 1. Read the source fully 2. Identify which existing pages it updates, contradicts, or extends 3. Update those pages 4. Create new pages if needed 5. Update index.md 6. Append to log.md

When a source contradicts an existing page, add a > ⚠️ Note: callout with the date and source, then flag the discrepancy clearly.

docs.title: Query workflow
docs.text: When answering a question: 1. Read index.md to find relevant pages 2. Read those pages 3. Synthesize answer with [[citations]] 4. If the answer is broadly useful, file it as a new wiki page

docs.title: Lint checklist (run periodically)

• [ ] Orphan pages (no inbound links from other pages)

docs.title: Content principles
docs.text:
For beginners: - Define every term on first use - Never assume they know what GBP, NFC, or white-label means - Lead with "what you do" before "how it works" - Use real examples from the community whenever possible

What to leave out: - Internal Climbo dev details (API internals, database schema) - Speculation about future features - Content that hasn't been confirmed working

Climbo Overview
docs.text:
One-line summary: Climbo is the white-label AI platform you use to run a reputation management agency — your clients see your brand, you keep the margin.

tags: [overview, core-concept] sources: [BRocA7husPU, sIGOLZ1kFAQ, PQiNrTKVLOU] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Climbo is a SaaS platform built for agency owners, not for local businesses directly. You pay €99/month flat. You rebrand the entire platform with your name, logo, and domain. You resell it to local businesses (restaurants, dentists, gyms, law firms, contractors) at whatever price you choose — typically $99–$500/month per client. Every client you add increases your profit; your cost stays fixed at €99.

The core promise to your clients: more Google reviews, higher ranking, more customers — all automated. They do not log in daily. They do not manage anything. The system runs in the background.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
Climbo runs what the founder Giacomo calls the Review Marketing Flywheel:

Customer leaves a review
  → SEO Agent replies with keyword-rich text (Google Maps ranking improves)
  → Social Agent turns the review into a social post (GBP stays active)
  → GEO Agent publishes blog/web content (client appears on ChatGPT, Perplexity)
  → More visibility → more customers → more reviews

This cycle runs automatically once you complete the initial setup. Setup takes roughly 1–2 hours per new client. After that, your time commitment per client per month is close to zero.

Three AI agents power the flywheel: - SEO Agent — automatically replies to Google reviews using the client's business name, city, and service keywords - Social Agent — turns the best reviews into image + caption posts on Google Business Profile (GBP), Facebook, and Instagram - GEO Agent — builds and updates a website/blog so the client gets cited by AI search engines like ChatGPT and Perplexity (released April 17, 2026)

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• GBP = Google Business Profile. It's the listing that appears when someone searches "dentist near me" on Google. This is the single most important asset for local businesses and the main thing Climbo manages.
  • Zero-gating: Climbo does NOT secretly redirect unhappy customers away from Google. Every reviewer can always leave a public review. This is compliant with Google's April 2026 policy update.
  • 21 review platforms supported: Google is the priority, but Climbo also connects to Tripadvisor, Yelp, Booking.com, and 17 others.
  • White-label means invisible: Your client sees your brand name, your domain, your AI assistant's name. Climbo is the engine; you are the face.
  • Break-even is one client. If you charge €100/month, you profit from client 2 onward.

docs.title: The math
docs.text:
Clients Your revenue Climbo cost Your profit 1 €100/mo €99/mo €1/mo 5 €500/mo €99/mo €401/mo 10 €1,000/mo €99/mo €901/mo 30 €3,000/mo €99/mo €2,901/mo

Giacomo's training shows agencies reaching $3,000/month (≈30 clients at $100) within a year using consistent outreach.

docs.title: GEO Agent
docs.text:
One-line summary: The GEO Agent builds and updates a structured website for your client so they appear when someone asks ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Google AI "best [service] in [city]."

tags: [ai-agents, geo, ai-search] sources: [LIn64wsy8wA, PLAYBOOK, skool-clients-outcomes] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
GEO stands for Generative Engine Optimization — the practice of making a business visible in AI search answers, not just traditional Google results.

When someone types "best Italian restaurant in Austin" into ChatGPT, the AI doesn't check Google's search index. It draws from structured content it can understand: websites with clear schema markup, accurate business data, and consistent information. The GEO Agent makes your clients' websites exactly that.

Released: April 17, 2026. This is Climbo's most recently launched major feature.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
The GEO Agent pulls data from the client's Google Business Profile and their accumulated reviews to automatically:

  1. Build a website for the client (if they don't have one) or update an existing one
  2. Generate blog articles using review content and local keywords
  3. Add correct schema markup (structured data that tells AI systems what the business is, where it is, what it does, its hours, rating, and services)
  4. Keep the website in sync with the GBP — if hours change on Google, the website updates automatically

The result: a website that Google understands, AI systems can cite, and potential customers trust.

Key technical detail: The website auto-includes LocalBusiness, Review, and service schema markup. This is what Giacomo's training calls "exploiting the power of reviews" — the review content is not just on Google; it becomes structured web content that feeds into AI answers.

docs.title: After connection
docs.text:
Once the GEO Agent is active, it works on two timescales:

  • Immediately: the website is generated with all existing GBP data and past reviews
  • Ongoing: every new review feeds a new blog post or website update, keeping content fresh

docs.title: What results look like
docs.text:
From a pinned Skool community post (July 2, 2026):

"This client came on board, paid the year in full about a month ago, but has been focused on website tweaks and branding and we hadn't managed to get socials and GBP connected. Officially got it all connected this morning and these are the results already:

Brand new business: No reviews as yet — #1 on ChatGPT — #1 on Gemini — #6 on Google Maps (out of 20+)"

This is a brand-new business with zero reviews. Just connecting the GBP and enabling the GEO Agent put them top of ChatGPT and Gemini within hours.

docs.title: How to enable it
docs.text:
1. Ensure the client's GBP is connected (this is required)
  2. Go to client account → AI Agents → GEO Agent
  3. Enable the agent
  4. Optionally: set the client's domain for the website if they want it hosted there
  5. The agent generates the initial website automatically

The client can preview their website from inside the platform and enable/disable specific sections (reviews, services, photos, hours, map, etc.) without touching any code.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• GEO Agent is the most differentiated feature in 2026. Competitors like BirdEye and traditional reputation management tools do not have this. It is a strong closer for premium plan tiers.
  • Zero reviews still works. The business data from GBP alone is enough to generate a structured website that AI can cite. Reviews supercharge it, but are not required to start.
  • Domain hosting: Climbo can host the generated website, or you can point a client's custom domain to it. Custom domain = more credibility.
  • AI Citation is verifiable. After enabling the GEO Agent, have the client ask ChatGPT "best [service] in [city]" in a private chat window. They will often see their business appear within days. This is the clearest ROI demonstration you can show.
  • Available as part of the standard €99/month agency plan.

docs.title: AI Agents Overview
docs.text:
One-line summary: Three AI agents run automatically in the background, turning each new review into SEO value, social content, and AI search visibility.

tags: [ai-agents, core-feature] sources: [BRocA7husPU, LIn64wsy8wA, sIGOLZ1kFAQ, 12NwL4G8vIE] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Climbo has three AI agents. You enable them during client onboarding and they run on autopilot forever after. Your client does not need to understand them — they just see the results.

The agents work together as a loop: every incoming review feeds all three agents, which together improve the client's visibility across Google, social media, and AI search engines.

New review arrives
  ↓
SEO Agent → AI reply with local keywords → Google Maps ranking improves
  ↓
Social Agent → review becomes a post on GBP, Facebook, Instagram
  ↓
GEO Agent → review content feeds blog article → ChatGPT/Perplexity citation
  ↓
More visibility → more customers → more reviews

This is the Review Marketing Flywheel. It compounds over time. A client with 10 reviews benefits less than one with 100, which benefits less than one with 500.

docs.title: SEO Agent
docs.text:
Automatically replies to every Google review using the client's business name, city, and main services as keywords. This is the most direct signal Google uses to rank local businesses.

Configurable settings: - Reply tone (professional, friendly, formal) - Reply length - Language (supports multiple languages) - Custom keywords to include - Additional instructions

→ Full details: [[ai-agents/seo-agent]]

docs.title: Social Agent
docs.text:
Takes the best incoming reviews and turns them into social media posts. Generates an image and a caption, then publishes to Google Business Profile posts, Facebook, and Instagram — automatically.

The GBP post is especially important: Google favors active profiles. Regular posts signal to Google that the business is engaged, which helps Maps ranking.

→ Full details: [[ai-agents/social-agent]]

docs.title: GEO Agent
docs.text:
Builds and updates a website/blog for the client using their review content and business data. This content is structured with schema markup so AI systems like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google's AI Overviews can cite the business.

Released April 17, 2026. This is the newest and most differentiated of the three agents.

→ Full details: [[ai-agents/geo-agent]]

docs.title: Where to configure agents
docs.text:
Agents are configured in two places: 1. Brand settings (agency-level defaults) — set the baseline behavior for all clients 2. Per-client settings — override defaults for individual clients

Go to Brand → SEO Agent / Social Agent / GEO Agent to set defaults. Then when you onboard a client, go to their account and adjust if needed.

docs.title: What the client sees
docs.text:
From the July 9, 2026 AMA (Home Charts feature), the client's home screen shows: - Agent Activity panel — a live log of what each agent has done (replies written, posts published, blog articles created) - Growth charts — review count over time, rating trend, GBP impressions and clicks

Clients see proof that the system is working without needing to understand the mechanics.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• You can run "reply to all past reviews" with one click — useful for clients who have 50+ unanswered reviews from years past
  • The AI name that appears to clients is your branded AI name (configured in Brand), not "Climbo AI"
  • If a client asks ChatGPT about their business type in their city and the business doesn't appear, the GEO Agent is the fix
  • Agents use tokens from your Balance. At $6.60 per 1M tokens, the cost is minimal for most clients

docs.title: SEO Agent
docs.text:
One-line summary: The SEO Agent automatically replies to every Google review using local keywords, improving the client's Maps ranking without any manual effort.

tags: [ai-agents, seo, reviews] sources: [BRocA7husPU, LIn64wsy8wA] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
When a customer leaves a review on Google, the SEO Agent writes and publishes an AI reply within minutes. The reply includes: - The client's business name - The city or neighborhood - The specific service mentioned in the review - A natural, human-sounding tone

This matters for ranking because Google's algorithm scores how actively a business responds to reviews. A business with 100 reviews and 100 replies ranks better than one with 100 reviews and 0 replies — all else equal.

A typical AI reply might look like:

"Thank you so much for your kind words, Maria! At [Business Name] in [City], we're passionate about [Service]. We're thrilled you had such a great experience and look forward to seeing you again!"

Simple. Relevant. Keyword-rich. Published instantly.

docs.title: Setup (one time per client)
docs.text:
1. Go to the client's account in Climbo
  2. Navigate to AI Agents → SEO Agent
  3. Enable the agent
  4. Configure:
  5. Keywords — list the most important ones (business name, city, services)
  6. Reply language — set to match the client's customers
  7. Reply length — short (1–2 sentences), medium (3–4 sentences), or long
  8. Tone — professional, friendly, formal, or custom prompt

  9. Additional instructions — any specific phrases to include or avoid

  10. Optionally: click "Reply to all past reviews" to retroactively reply to existing unanswered reviews. This one action can have an immediate positive effect on Maps ranking.

docs.title: Ongoing operation
docs.text:
Once enabled, the agent fires automatically every time a new review arrives. No action needed from you or the client.

The client can see all agent-generated replies in the Reviews section of their dashboard, and can edit or override any reply manually if they prefer a different tone for a specific review.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• Replying to negative reviews is just as important as positive ones. A graceful, professional reply to a 1-star review shows potential customers that the business cares. The SEO Agent handles these too, but you may want to review negative replies before they go live. Check the agent settings for a "require approval for negative reviews" option.
  • Language matters. If your client serves a French-speaking market, set the language to French. The reply will match the reviewer's expectation.
  • Custom prompt = full control. You can write a custom prompt to match the exact voice of the business. Example: "Reply as a warm, family-run business. Always mention that we've been serving [City] since 2010."
  • Keywords are not spam. The SEO Agent weaves keywords naturally. It does not keyword-stuff. Google can detect unnatural keyword insertion and it hurts rather than helps.
  • The agent uses AI tokens from your Balance. For a client getting 30 reviews/month, token cost is a few cents.

docs.title: Common question: Is this compliant?
docs.text:
Yes. Replying to reviews is explicitly encouraged by Google. There is no policy against AI-assisted replies as long as the replies are genuine and relevant — which the SEO Agent is designed to produce.

The agent does NOT offer incentives in exchange for reviews, does NOT post reviews, and does NOT manipulate review content. It only responds.

docs.title: Social Agent
docs.text:
One-line summary: The Social Agent turns your client's best reviews into social media posts and publishes them automatically to Google Business Profile, Facebook, and Instagram.

tags: [ai-agents, social-media, gbp] sources: [BRocA7husPU, LIn64wsy8wA] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Social Agent does three things automatically when a new five-star review arrives: 1. Generates a branded image featuring the review text 2. Writes a caption for the post 3. Publishes to Google Business Profile (GBP), Facebook, and/or Instagram

No scheduling. No manual posting. No copywriter needed.

This matters for two reasons: - GBP ranking: Google favors active profiles. A business that posts weekly ranks above one that posted once in 2022. The Social Agent keeps the profile alive continuously. - Social proof: Potential customers browsing Instagram or Facebook see real five-star reviews from real customers. This is social proof that converts.

docs.title: Setup (one time per client)
docs.text:
1. In the client's account, go to AI Agents → Social Agent
  2. Enable the agent
  3. Connect social platforms:
  4. Google Business Profile — already connected from GBP setup
  5. Facebook — client logs in via Facebook OAuth
  6. Instagram — connected via Facebook Business Manager
  7. Configure:
  8. Which platforms to publish to
  9. Post frequency (publish every review? or cap at X posts per week?)
  10. Caption style (promotional, friendly, question-based, etc.)
  11. Custom prompt for captions if you want a specific voice

docs.title: Ongoing operation
docs.text:
When a four or five-star review arrives, the agent: 1. Selects it as eligible for a post (lower-rated reviews are skipped) 2. Creates an image with the review text, star rating, and business branding 3. Writes a caption using the configured style 4. Publishes to all connected platforms simultaneously

The client can see all social posts in their platform under the Social or Content section. Posts go live without any approval step unless you configure otherwise.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• GBP posts are the most important output. Even if the client doesn't care about Facebook, GBP posts directly influence local Maps ranking. Always enable GBP at minimum.
  • Image quality is automatic. Climbo generates images that look like branded marketing materials. Clients who see the first auto-generated post are usually surprised how good it looks.
  • You can customize the visual style. The image template follows your brand settings, so if you've set brand colors in Climbo, those colors appear in the generated images.
  • Frequency cap. If a client gets 20 reviews in a week, you probably don't want 20 posts published in that same week. Configure a reasonable cap (e.g., 3 posts/week maximum) to keep posting natural.
  • Caption approval mode. If a client is particular about messaging, enable an approval step so they (or you) can review captions before they publish.

docs.title: What clients see
docs.text:
From their dashboard, clients can see: - A gallery of all auto-published social posts - Which platform each post was sent to - Engagement stats if connected via Facebook/Instagram API - The SEO Agent activity alongside social activity in the Home Charts view

When a client sees that their Instagram profile now has 30 posts they never wrote, using real five-star reviews from real customers, the value of the platform is obvious.

docs.title: Community Wins
docs.text:
One-line summary: Real stories from Climbo agency owners — what they did, what they got, and what you can learn from each.

tags: [case-studies, wins, community] sources: [skool-simple-yet-effective, skool-our-first-paying-client, skool-fortune-favours-the-brave, skool-it-took-some-time, skool-from-birdeye-to-climbo, skool-win-4, skool-clients-outcomes, skool-landed-first-client] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: These are real posts from the Climbo Accelerator Skool community (933 members, Nov 2025–Jul 2026). Names and situations are as posted. Use these as models, not scripts.

docs.title: 1. "4 clients in the most simplistic way" — Jan 13 2026
docs.text:
A member in the UK posted exactly what he did: posted in a local business Facebook group saying he had a solution to help businesses grow on Google through AI-powered reviews. He offered 14 days free, including a "review reactivation" where he'd send messages to their past customers. No fancy pitch. No cold calls.

He got 4 clients from a single Facebook post.

Lesson: Local Facebook groups are warm. Business owners already know they need reviews. A free trial offer in the right place converts without selling.

docs.title: 2. "Our first paying client" — Jan 22 2026
docs.text:
A member who signed up in May 2025 but spent months dragging their feet out of fear. She finally started by approaching a Thai restaurant for a free trial. Then she pitched a French bakery in Fort Lauderdale during her anniversary trip. Then a hibachi and sushi restaurant back home. Her strategy: target businesses where English isn't the first language, because those owners respond especially well to AI replies in their customers' native tongue.

Three trials turned into paying clients.

Lesson: Fear is the only thing stopping most people. The first "yes" from a free trial client is the hardest. The next two come faster.

docs.title: 3. "Fortune favours the brave" — Jan 30 2026
docs.text:
A member who had been going to the same pizza place for seven years. He finally introduced himself to the owner before Christmas. One evening, with help from another Climber's post, he set up an NFC and QR code stand before heading in. He didn't even have an active Climbo account yet. The owner was impressed on the spot.

Lesson: Your best first clients are people you already have a relationship with. The NFC stand is a physical demo — it shows instead of tells.

docs.title: 4. "2.5 months then BOOM" — Mar 13 2026
docs.text:
A member who focused on restaurants and cafés. He spent 2.5 months getting rejected — owners who'd been contacted by Google and other directories before and were tired of pitches. He started to doubt his niche. He kept going. Then in one week, he closed 5 restaurants on annual plans.

"3.000 € in one week is pretty awesome."

Lesson: The pipeline builds slowly, then converts in batches. Staying in one niche (restaurants) means you get better at the pitch over time. The owners who say no in month one often come back.

docs.title: 5. "Landed first client and 3 demos booked" — Mar 2 2026
docs.text:
A member posted a single sentence: landed first client and has three demos booked. 32 comments of celebration followed. Sometimes the win is that simple — you close one, and the momentum is real.

Lesson: The first client is a milestone worth sharing. The community celebrates it because they've all been there. That energy is real fuel.

docs.title: 6. "From BirdEye to Climbo" — May 22 2026
docs.text:
Wayne Connell (one of the community's top contributors) landed a new client who was previously paying for BirdEye — one of the largest and oldest reputation management platforms. BirdEye has hundreds of thousands of clients and has been in the market for decades.

Giacomo posted about it directly in the community: "Every business currently on BirdEye can level up and join Climbo. Go get them."

Lesson: You are not only competing against "no one has heard of review management." You're winning deals away from established incumbents. Position Climbo as the modern replacement.

docs.title: 7. "Win #4 — Hat Trick" — Jun 19 2026
docs.text:
A member and his colleague landed their first customer with three locations. During the World Cup they called it a hat trick. One client relationship, three GBP connections, three times the monthly value.

Lesson: Multi-location clients multiply your revenue without multiplying your onboarding work. Ask every client how many locations they have before quoting.

docs.title: 8. "#1 on ChatGPT, day one" — Jul 2 2026 (pinned post)
docs.text:
A member's client had paid for a year in full but spent a month on website tweaks without connecting their GBP. When they finally connected everything in one morning, the results came immediately. Brand new business, zero reviews yet:

"Number 1 on ChatGPT. Number 1 on Gemini. Number 6 on Google Maps (out of 20+)."

Just from connecting the GBP and activating the GEO Agent. No reviews yet.

Lesson: The GEO Agent starts working before you have reviews. Connecting GBP on day one is the single most important step. Don't wait for reviews to set up AI optimization.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• Free trials are the most common conversion path — show value before asking for payment
  • Multi-location clients are the best-value deals (one relationship, multiple fees)
  • The first client is the hardest; the pattern repeats much faster afterward
  • Sharing wins in the Skool community generates feedback and accountability

docs.title: GDPR — EU Compliance for Review Requests
docs.text:
One-line summary: Practical guidance for EU agency owners sending review requests — what you need to know, not legal advice.

tags: [compliance, gdpr, eu, email, sms] sources: [skool-gdpr-compliant, skool-post-ama-promise] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
If you're operating in the EU, GDPR applies when you collect and use customer data (names, phone numbers, email addresses) to send review requests. This page covers the basics you need to stay safe in practice.

This is not legal advice. If you have specific concerns, consult a lawyer in your country.

docs.title: What GDPR means for your Climbo setup
docs.text:
When your clients give you a list of their past customers to upload into Climbo, those customers' data (name, email, phone) is being processed. As the agency running Climbo:

  • Your client is the data controller — they collected that data from their customers
  • You are the data processor — you're processing it on their behalf
  • You should have a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) in place with each client (a short contract addendum)

docs.title: Lawful basis for sending review requests
docs.text:
The most practical lawful basis for a review request is legitimate interest or an existing customer relationship (in some EU countries, this is called the "soft opt-in" rule under ePrivacy).

In practice, this means:

  • Only send review requests to people who have had a real transaction with the business — not cold contacts, not scraped lists
  • The request must be related to the transaction (asking about their experience as a customer)
  • The business name must be clearly visible in the message so the recipient knows who is contacting them

docs.title: Practical checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Only import customers who have made a purchase or appointment
  • [ ] Review request templates include the business name (Climbo's default templates do)
  • [ ] You have a DPA with your clients
  • [ ] Clients understand they are responsible for having had a legitimate relationship with their customers
  • [ ] Do not purchase contact lists or send to unrelated contacts

docs.title: What about SMS?
docs.text: SMS (text messages) for marketing is more tightly regulated in some EU countries than email. In some jurisdictions you need explicit opt-in consent for promotional SMS. Review requests generally qualify as transactional, not promotional, but this varies by country. When in doubt, start with email.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• GDPR came up as one of the highest-engagement threads in the Skool community (28 comments, Jan 2026) — it is a real concern for EU agency owners
  • Climbo's data is stored in their infrastructure; ask Climbo support directly for their DPA if you need one for your clients
  • The "legitimate interest" basis is generally sufficient for post-transaction review requests to existing customers — you do not need prior opt-in consent in most EU countries for this specific use case

docs.title: Google Review Policy 2026
docs.text:
One-line summary: Google banned review gating in April 2026. Climbo is compliant by design — here's what this means for you and your clients.

tags: [compliance, google, reviews] sources: [i4D5yXclzEY, yAx4pzDTmsY, skool-google-review-policy-compliance] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
In April 2026, Google updated its Google Business Profile (GBP) Terms of Service to explicitly ban review gating — the practice of pre-filtering customers before sending them to leave a Google review.

Review gating means: showing unhappy customers a private feedback form, and only directing happy customers to Google. Many agencies and competitor tools have been doing this for years. As of April 2026, it is a TOS violation and can result in GBP suspension.

Climbo does not gate. This is a competitive advantage.

docs.title: What changed
docs.text:
• Banned: Sending customers through a "happy/unhappy" filter before the review link
  • Banned: Routing negative respondents to a private form while only positive ones reach Google
  • Allowed: Asking all customers for reviews (universally, without pre-screening)
  • Allowed: Suggesting wording to customers who choose to leave a review (Climbo's AI Review Suggestion does exactly this — it helps happy customers write better reviews, but it does not filter who receives the link)

docs.title: Climbo's position
docs.text:
Climbo sends the review request link to all customers. There is no happy/unhappy gate. The AI Review Suggestion feature (launched Feb 2026) helps customers who are already writing a review to craft a better one — it does not filter who gets asked.

This means you will occasionally collect 3-star reviews. That is correct behavior.

docs.title: What to tell clients
docs.text:
"You will get all your reviews, including occasional 3-star ones. That's actually more trustworthy to Google and to potential customers. A business with 150 reviews averaging 4.3 stars looks more credible than one with 12 reviews at 5.0. We keep you safe and compliant."

Many competitors still gate. If a prospect says "another tool only sends happy customers to Google," that tool is now violating Google's TOS and putting the client's GBP at risk.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• This policy update was heavily discussed in the Skool community (46 comments in May 2026 — one of the highest-engagement threads)
  • Use "zero-gating compliance" as a sales talking point when comparing against other tools
  • If a client pressures you to filter reviews, explain the suspension risk — it protects them and you

docs.title: Balance Explained
docs.text:
One-line summary: Balance is your pre-paid credit pool that covers the cost of SMS messages, WhatsApp messages, and AI tokens used across all your clients.

tags: [features, billing, balance] sources: [PLAYBOOK, YOUTUBE-ANALYSIS] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Your Climbo Balance is separate from your €99/month subscription. It is a pre-paid credit system — similar to a phone top-up. You add money to your balance, and Climbo deducts from it as you use SMS, WhatsApp, and AI agents.

Email is free and does not touch your balance.

docs.title: What uses balance
docs.text:
Channel / Feature Approximate cost SMS (EU/US) €0.05–0.10 per message WhatsApp ~$0.03 per message Email Free AI Tokens (SEO/Social/GEO agents) $6.60 per 1 million tokens

Real-world context: - A client getting 50 review requests per month via SMS = approximately $2.50–5.00/month in balance - A client with 30 incoming reviews/month (SEO Agent replies each) = roughly $0.10–0.20 in AI tokens - AI token costs are minimal for most use cases

docs.title: Adding balance
docs.text: Go to Settings → Balance in your agency dashboard and add credit via card. You can set a minimum threshold and enable auto-recharge if you want to avoid running out unexpectedly.

docs.title: How balance is shared
docs.text:
Your single balance pool covers all your clients. If you have 20 clients all sending SMS review requests, they all draw from the same pool. There is no per-client allocation by default.

June 2026 update: AI Token Allocation per client was released, allowing you to assign a specific token budget to individual clients. This prevents one high-volume client from consuming disproportionate resources.

docs.title: Choosing the right channel per client
docs.text:
• Email → free, good for B2B clients or when phone numbers aren't collected
  • SMS → high open rates (90%+), ideal for restaurants and high-traffic local businesses
  • WhatsApp → highest engagement in many markets, requires WhatsApp Business number setup by client
  • QR/NFC → no balance cost at all (review request is built into the physical display)

For clients on free trials or in early stages, QR/NFC + email-only setup has zero variable costs. Upgrade to SMS/WhatsApp once you're billing them.

docs.title: Monitoring balance
docs.text: Your agency dashboard shows your current balance. You can also see usage logs — how many messages were sent, to whom, at what cost. This helps you understand the actual cost per client and whether your pricing covers it.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• Top up before activating your first SMS-heavy client. With a starting balance of $5, you have maybe 50–100 SMS messages. If your client sends requests to 200 customers on day one, you'll run out mid-campaign.
  • For early-stage agencies (1–5 clients): $20 balance is sufficient for months. Revisit as you scale.
  • Balance costs are your cost of goods sold. Factor them into your per-client profitability: if you charge $100/month and spend $5/month on SMS for that client, your margin is $95 — still excellent.
  • WhatsApp Business number: Before enabling WhatsApp requests for a client, they need to have a verified WhatsApp Business number. This is set up in their integrations, not in your balance settings.

docs.title: Client Portal
docs.text:
One-line summary: The client portal is what your clients log into — a fully branded dashboard showing their review stats, rankings, agent activity, and GBP performance.

tags: [features, client-portal, dashboard] sources: [BRocA7husPU, 12NwL4G8vIE] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
When your client logs into app.youragency.com (your branded domain), they land on the client portal. This is their view — not your agency view.

The portal shows your client: - How many reviews they have and how the count is growing - Their Google rating trend - What the AI agents have been doing - How their GBP is performing (impressions, clicks, calls) - Where they rank versus competitors

Clients do not see billing settings, other clients' data, or any reference to Climbo.

docs.title: Home screen (updated July 9, 2026 — Home Charts)
docs.text:
The home screen is divided into two sections after the July 2026 update:

Agent Activity (right side): - Live log of what each agent has done - "SEO Agent replied to 3 reviews today" - "Social Agent published 2 posts to GBP and Instagram" - "GEO Agent updated the website with 1 new article"

Growth Charts (left side): Three charts that update monthly, tracked from the day the client joined your platform:

  1. Reviews chart — total review count over time (blue line) + average rating (orange line)
  2. Rankings chart — Google Maps position for key search terms, compared to competitors
  3. GBP Performance chart — impressions, clicks, direction requests, call clicks

Giacomo designed these charts specifically for retention: "Your clients buy results, not features. These charts show them that since they joined your platform, the most important KPIs are improving month over month."

docs.title: Other portal sections
docs.text:
Reviews: - Inbox of all reviews across connected platforms - Private feedback submissions from unhappy customers - Ability to reply manually (or see AI replies already sent)

Contacts: - Add customer contacts manually - Upload CSV for bulk import - View auto-send status for each contact

Requests: - Send quick review request - View request history and open/click rates

Google Business Profile: - Edit business info (syncs to Google) - Upload photos - Publish posts and offers

AI Agents: - View or adjust agent settings - See agent activity log

Settings: - Connect integrations (Google, Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp) - Manage review templates

docs.title: AI Chatbot alternative
docs.text:
If you enable the AI Chatbot in your Brand settings, clients see a chat window instead of (or alongside) the full dashboard. They can ask their AI assistant to: - "Send a review request to my last customer" - "Update my business hours to 9am–7pm" - "Add a new contact: John Smith, phone 555-1234"

This is particularly useful for clients who find dashboards overwhelming. The chatbot interface removes the learning curve entirely.

docs.title: What good portal design looks like for retention
docs.text:
From the community: clients stay when they can clearly see that the number is going up. The "before" screenshot you take on onboarding day becomes the evidence you show at day 90: - "You had 12 reviews. You now have 47." - "Your Google Maps ranking moved from position 8 to position 2 for 'dentist in [city].'" - "Your GBP got 430 more impressions this month than last month."

These are the moments that lock in a long-term relationship.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• Set the baseline on day 1. Screenshot the starting stats before anything runs. You need a before picture to show the after.
  • Teach the client where to look. During onboarding, walk them through the dashboard. Show them the home charts. Tell them "this number will go up every week."
  • The gamification element. Climbo's review stats show "you need X more five-star reviews to reach 4.8 stars" — clients compete against themselves to hit the next milestone.
  • Sentiment analysis. Monthly reports analyze the text of all reviews to identify what customers love and what needs improvement. This is genuinely useful for the business, not just for marketing.

docs.title: GBP Integration
docs.text:
One-line summary: Connecting Google Business Profile (GBP) is the foundation of everything — reviews, replies, social posts, ranking, and the GEO Agent all depend on it.

tags: [features, gbp, google] sources: [BRocA7husPU] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
GBP (Google Business Profile) is the free Google listing that appears when someone searches for a local business. It shows the name, address, hours, photos, reviews, and contact info. It is the most important digital asset a local business has.

Climbo connects directly to GBP via the Google API. Once connected: - New reviews appear in Climbo automatically - The SEO Agent can publish replies directly to Google - The Social Agent can create GBP posts - Business info edited in Climbo syncs to Google instantly - The GEO Agent can generate a website that matches GBP data exactly

docs.title: Connecting GBP
docs.text:
During client onboarding: 1. Go to client account → Settings → Integrations → Google 2. Click "Connect Google Account" 3. The client logs in with the Google account that manages their GBP 4. Select the correct GBP location if they have multiple 5. Done — connection takes about 60 seconds

If the client cannot be present, you can request Manager access on their GBP instead: - Go to Google Business Profile manager → add a new Manager using your Google account - Once accepted, you can connect the GBP from your Climbo without needing the client's login credentials - This also protects you: if the client churns, remove your access. If you own the Manager role, you maintain control.

docs.title: What Climbo manages via GBP
docs.text: Once connected, from inside the client's Climbo dashboard: - Business info — name, address, phone, website, hours, social links, services, attributes — any edit here syncs to Google automatically - Photos — add or update photos; they appear on GBP - Special hours — holiday hours, temporary closures - Google Posts — publish updates, offers, events directly to GBP (Social Agent does this automatically; client or you can also post manually) - Reviews — see and reply to all Google reviews in one place

docs.title: Why accurate GBP info matters
docs.text:
If the hours on GBP say "open until 8pm" but the business closes at 6pm, customers arrive at a locked door and leave a 1-star review. If the website says one phone number but GBP says another, Google sees inconsistency and penalizes rankings.

Climbo's GBP integration ensures the info stays accurate. Any change made in Climbo flows to Google and also updates the auto-generated website (if the GEO Agent is active), keeping everything consistent.

This consistency is a direct ranking factor: Google trusts businesses whose information matches across their listing and their website.

docs.title: Multiple locations
docs.text: If a client has more than one location (e.g., a chain of gyms, a franchise), each location is a separate client profile in Climbo. You onboard each location individually. Some community members manage clients with 15+ locations this way.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• GBP must be claimed. If the client's Google listing is unclaimed (no manager), help them claim it first at business.google.com. This is free and takes a few days for Google to verify.
  • Suspended or flagged GBPs. If a client's GBP has been suspended, Climbo cannot fix that. You'll need to work with Google's reinstatement process separately.
  • One GBP per Climbo client profile. Do not connect two different GBPs to the same client account — create separate profiles.
  • Instant sync. The connection is live. Reviews appear in Climbo within minutes of being posted on Google.
  • Other review platforms. Climbo supports 21 review platforms total. Connect Tripadvisor, Yelp, Booking.com, etc. from the same Integrations section. Google stays the priority.

docs.title: Google Sheets Integration
docs.text:
One-line summary: Connect a Google Sheet to Climbo so new rows automatically add contacts and trigger review requests — no CRM required.

tags: [features, integrations, automation] sources: [BRocA7husPU, YOUTUBE-ANALYSIS, SKOOL-ANALYSIS] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Google Sheets integration lets you use a simple spreadsheet as a CRM that feeds Climbo automatically. When a business owner adds a customer to their Google Sheet (name, phone, service date), Climbo picks it up via Zapier and automatically sends that customer a review request at the right time.

Released: May 2026. 24 comments in the Skool community on release.

docs.title: The flow
docs.text:
Business owner adds new row to Google Sheet
  → Zapier detects new row
  → Zapier adds contact to Climbo
  → Climbo auto-send triggers: sends review request X hours after service date

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
What you need: - A Google Sheet with columns: Name, Phone Number (or Email), Service Date - A Zapier account (free tier is sufficient) - Climbo's Zapier integration enabled (Settings → Zapier)

Step-by-step:

  1. In Climbo: go to Settings → Zapier → generate your Zapier API key
  2. In Zapier: create a new Zap
  3. Trigger: Google Sheets → New Row
  4. Action: Climbo → Add Contact
  5. Map columns: Sheet Name → Contact Name, Sheet Phone → Contact Phone, Sheet Date → Service Date
  6. Enable auto-send in Climbo (set trigger: "send X hours after service date")
  7. Test: add a row to the sheet, confirm the contact appears in Climbo within a few minutes

docs.title: The direct Google Sheets integration (May 2026)
docs.text:
Climbo also released a native Google Sheets integration (separate from Zapier) in May 2026. This allows client data — review counts, ratings, rankings — to sync out to a Google Sheet automatically. Use cases:

  • Client reporting: Share a Google Sheet with your client instead of asking them to log into the portal — they see their data in a familiar format
  • Agency tracking: One master sheet showing all clients' review counts, updated automatically
  • Custom dashboards: Build Google Looker Studio dashboards from the Sheets data

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• Perfect for technophobe clients. If a client refuses to learn a dashboard but already uses Google Sheets daily, this integration gives them everything they need without changing their workflow.
  • The sheet is their CRM. Train the client: "After every appointment, add the customer to row X. Everything else is automatic."
  • Column order matters in Zapier. Map your columns carefully during Zap setup. A mismapped phone field sends review requests to the wrong number.
  • Date format matters. Make sure the service date column uses a format Zapier recognizes (YYYY-MM-DD is safest). Wrong date format = auto-send fires at the wrong time or not at all.
  • You don't need Zapier if the client manually uploads CSVs. Climbo accepts CSV contact uploads directly. For clients who prefer batch uploads over live sync, this is an easier path.

docs.title: Mobile App
docs.text:
One-line summary: Climbo has iOS and Android apps for both agency owners and clients to manage reviews and check stats on the go.

tags: [features, mobile] sources: [PQiNrTKVLOU, YOUTUBE-ANALYSIS] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Climbo released its iOS and Android apps in April 2026. Both the agency view and the client view are available on mobile.

For clients, this is especially important: they can see their review count growing in real time on their phone. A notification arriving that says "You got a new 5-star review" — without them doing anything — is one of the strongest retention signals in the product.

docs.title: For clients (local business owners)
docs.text:
The mobile app gives clients: - Review notifications — push notification every time a new review arrives - Stats at a glance — current rating, total reviews, recent activity - Quick requests — send a review request to a customer directly from the phone - AI agent activity — see that the SEO Agent has replied and the Social Agent has posted

Most clients will use the app only for the notification and the occasional stats check. They are not managing anything; they are watching the number go up.

docs.title: For agency owners
docs.text:
From the app you can: - Monitor all clients' activity - See incoming reviews across your client base - Check MRR trends - Respond to client questions

The mobile experience is lighter than the full desktop dashboard but covers the essentials for quick checks while away from your computer.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
• iOS: available on the App Store (search "Climbo")
  • Android: available on Google Play Store
  • Clients access the app using their credentials for your branded platform

Note: The app may show the Climbo name during installation (App Store listing) even though the platform inside is white-labeled. This is a current limitation — clients who search the App Store will see "Climbo." Some agencies address this by directing clients to the mobile web version of their custom domain instead.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• Push notifications are a retention tool. A restaurant owner who gets a notification at 9pm saying "3 new reviews today!" on their phone is a client who stays. Make sure clients install the app during onboarding.
  • Mobile web works too. If a client doesn't want to install an app, your custom domain at app.youragency.com works well on mobile browsers.
  • Agency view on mobile. Useful for checking in on clients while traveling, but full configuration and setup is easier on desktop.
  • Announced and released: April 2026 (iOS and Android simultaneously).

docs.title: NFC Cards
docs.text:
One-line summary: NFC cards let customers tap their phone to a physical card or stand and instantly open the review page — no QR scan, no typing.

tags: [features, nfc, review-collection] sources: [BRocA7husPU, skool-bulk-nfc, skool-fortune] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
NFC (Near Field Communication) is the same technology that powers contactless payments. When a customer holds their phone near an NFC card or sticker, their phone automatically opens a webpage — in this case, your client's review link.

No app download needed. No QR code scan. No typing. Just tap and review.

For businesses where staff interacts closely with customers (restaurants, beauty salons, personal trainers, clinics), NFC is often the highest-converting review collection method.

docs.title: What you need
docs.text:
• An NFC card, tag, sticker, or countertop stand (purchased separately — not included in Climbo)
  • A device to write (encode) the review URL to the NFC tag
  • The client's unique review link from Climbo

docs.title: Encoding the NFC tag
docs.text:
You write the client's Climbo review link onto the NFC tag using a free app like NFC Tools (iOS/Android):

  1. Get the client's review link from Climbo (Settings → Review Link)
  2. Open NFC Tools on your phone
  3. Select "Write" → "Add a Record" → "URL/URI"
  4. Paste the client's review link
  5. Hold the phone over the NFC card/sticker — encoding takes 1 second
  6. Test it: hold a customer's phone to the card and confirm the review page opens

docs.title: Placement
docs.text:
Physical options: - Countertop stand — best for reception areas - Tabletop sticker — good for restaurant tables - Business card — can be handed directly to customers - Framed card near the exit — catches customers as they leave

The community has successfully laminated NFC cards without losing functionality.

docs.title: Bulk encoding
docs.text: One community post with 31 comments asked about bulk NFC encoding. The short answer: yes, you can pre-encode multiple cards in batches before a client visit, making setup faster. If you serve many clients, buying NFC tags in bulk (e.g., 100 at a time) significantly reduces the per-card cost.

docs.title: The "Fortune Favours the Brave" story
docs.text:
One community member shared this before even having an active Climbo account:

"I set up this NFC & QR Code stand before going in. The boss just happened to [be there]. I showed him how it worked by tapping my own phone. His eyes lit up." (Skool, 28 comments)

The NFC demo is often the WOW moment in a sales pitch. You tap your phone. The review page opens instantly. The business owner has never seen anything like it. That moment — not the feature list, not the pricing — is often what closes the deal.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• Both NFC and QR code simultaneously. Climbo's kiosk page has a QR code printed on it. If you place an NFC stand, also laminate the QR code nearby — some phones or cases block NFC, so having both ensures no customer is left without an option.
  • Lamination is fine. Multiple community members have confirmed that laminating the card does not affect NFC functionality.
  • iOS: iPhones from iPhone 7 onward support NFC without any app. Android phones have had NFC for years. Coverage is effectively universal.
  • You can encode to the Climbo review link or directly to Google's review form. Sending directly to Google skips the rating screen. This is simpler but means all customers (happy or not) go straight to Google. If your client has a great reputation, this is fine. If they're still building it, use the Climbo review link first.
  • NFC tags are cheap. A 10-pack of plain NFC stickers (NTAG213 or NTAG215) costs under $5. Pre-encode several for each client.

docs.title: Review Requests
docs.text:
One-line summary: Review requests are the messages you send to customers asking them to leave a review — the fuel that starts the entire flywheel.

tags: [features, review-collection, core] sources: [BRocA7husPU] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Getting more reviews starts with asking for them. Most businesses don't ask, so they don't get them. Climbo gives your clients three ways to ask — choose the one that fits the business type.

This is the most important part of the setup. Giacomo's training states: "The review collection process is 90% of the sales. If you set it up correctly, they will get results during the free trial. If not, nothing else matters."

docs.title: Method 1 — QR Code / Kiosk Mode
docs.text:
Best for: high-traffic physical locations (restaurants, salons, gyms, retail)

Print the Climbo-generated kiosk page and place it at your client's reception, counter, or checkout area. Customers scan the QR code and land on the review link.

Setup: 1. Go to client account → Review Link → customize with client's logo and message 2. Go to Kiosk Mode → print the page (or download as PDF) 3. Laminate it. Place it where customers will see it after their experience. 4. Train the staff to say: "If you enjoyed your visit, scan the code — takes 30 seconds."

docs.title: Method 2 — Manual Quick Request
docs.text:
Best for: low-volume professional services (lawyers, dentists, accountants, consultants)

After each appointment, the client sends a one-tap review request directly to that customer.

Setup: 1. Create a WhatsApp or SMS template in Request → Templates 2. Example template: "Hi [Name], thank you for visiting us today. If you have a moment, we'd love your feedback: [Review Link]" 3. After each appointment: Request → Send → Quick Request → select contact → send

The platform shows open rates and click rates so you can see if the message is reaching people.

docs.title: Method 3 — Auto-Send (CRM Integration)
docs.text:
Best for: businesses with a CRM or contact list, or those willing to use Climbo as their CRM

When a new contact is added (manually or via automation), the platform automatically sends a review request after a configurable delay (e.g., 15 minutes after the service date).

Setup: 1. Go to Auto Send and enable the automation 2. Set the trigger: "Send request X hours after service date" 3. Select the template to use 4. Connect a CRM via Zapier, or have the client add contacts manually (or upload CSV)

Google Sheets integration: If the client tracks customers in a Google Sheet, Zapier can push new rows to Climbo automatically. Every new row = new review request sent. Zero manual work after setup.

docs.title: The review link
docs.text:
All three methods point to the same place: the review link. This is the unique URL Climbo generates for each client.

When a customer opens the link, they see: - The client's logo and name - A simple rating prompt (1–5 stars) - 1–3 stars → option to leave private feedback to the business (not public) - 4–5 stars → directed to leave a public review on Google (or other connected platforms)

This is compliant. Both paths are available to every customer. See [[compliance/google-review-policy-2026]].

Clients can also enable video testimonials from the review link — customers can record a short video review directly.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• Train the staff, not just the system. The QR code sitting on the counter does nothing if the employee never mentions it. Build a verbal habit: "Would you mind scanning the QR code for us?"
  • Send within 24 hours of the experience. Review request open rates drop sharply after 48 hours.
  • Multiple platforms. The review link can offer choices beyond Google — Yelp, Tripadvisor, Booking.com, etc. Focus on Google first unless the industry has a dominant niche platform.
  • Reminders. If a customer doesn't click the link, a follow-up reminder can be scheduled automatically. Enable this in template settings.
  • Complaint Link. Climbo also offers a dedicated complaint link — a separate URL you can share with customers who had a bad experience, giving them a private channel. This reduces public negative reviews without gating.

docs.title: Stripe Billing
docs.text:
One-line summary: Climbo connects to your Stripe account so client subscriptions are billed automatically — no invoices to chase, no manual renewals.

tags: [features, billing, stripe] sources: [PLAYBOOK, YOUTUBE-ANALYSIS, skool-first-client-stripe] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Climbo integrates with Stripe for client billing. Once connected, your clients enter their payment details once during signup and are billed automatically on a recurring schedule. You receive the money in your Stripe account. You do not handle any payment manually.

This is what makes the agency model truly passive: clients pay forever without you lifting a finger.

docs.title: Connecting Stripe
docs.text:
1. Go to Settings → Finance → Account in your Climbo agency dashboard
  2. Click "Connect Stripe" — you'll be redirected to Stripe's OAuth flow
  3. Log in to your Stripe account (or create one at stripe.com if you don't have one)
  4. Authorize Climbo to create subscriptions on your behalf
  5. Done — Stripe is now connected

docs.title: Creating plans with Stripe
docs.text:
When you create a Plan in Climbo (Plans → New Plan), you set: - Plan name - Monthly or annual price - Features included - Trial period (optional — e.g., 14 days free before first charge)

Climbo automatically creates the corresponding Stripe product and price object. When a client subscribes to your plan, a Stripe subscription is created. Billing is automatic.

docs.title: The client checkout experience
docs.text:
When a new client signs up or upgrades, they see a Stripe-hosted checkout page with: - Your agency's branding (colors, logo — configured in your Stripe account under Branding) - The plan details and price - Credit card or SEPA debit input fields - Optional: coupon code field (if you've created coupons in Climbo)

After payment, their account is automatically activated or upgraded.

docs.title: Coupons and free trials
docs.text:
Create coupons in Settings → Finance → Coupons: - 100% discount forever — for founding clients or special offers - % off for X months — for introductory promotions - Fixed amount off — for one-time discounts

Clients enter the coupon code at checkout. Stripe applies the discount automatically.

For a free trial without a coupon: set a trial period on the plan itself (e.g., 14-day free trial — card required at signup but not charged until the trial ends).

docs.title: The Sales dashboard (Home Charts)
docs.text:
In your agency dashboard under Sales, you see: - MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue) for the last 7, 30, 90, and 365 days - Individual subscription activity - Revenue trends

After the July 9, 2026 update (Home Charts), this view became significantly more detailed.

docs.title: Payouts
docs.text: Configure how you receive money in Settings → Finance → Payouts. Stripe pays out to your bank account on your configured schedule (daily, weekly, or monthly). Make sure your bank details are accurate before your first client is charged.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• Stripe is not available everywhere. If you operate in a country where Stripe is not supported, you'll need to handle billing manually or use a Stripe alternative connected via Zapier/Pabbly. Multiple community members in non-Stripe countries have found workarounds.
  • VAT for EU operators. Stripe may require a VAT number during integration for businesses in the EU. If you don't have one, check the Climbo community for workarounds specific to your country.
  • Stripe branding. The Stripe checkout page can show your brand colors and logo. Configure this in your Stripe account under Settings → Branding — it does not sync automatically from Climbo's brand settings.
  • Moving clients from free to paid. If you add a client manually (without Stripe), you can later transition them to a paid Stripe plan. The community has discussed the smoothest way to do this — generally create a private, non-public plan and share the checkout link directly.
  • The "First client with Stripe" moment is a community milestone. When the first automatic payment hits your Stripe dashboard, the business model becomes real. (This post got 39 comments in the Skool community.)

docs.title: White-Label
docs.text:
One-line summary: White-label means your clients see your brand entirely — your name, your domain, your AI assistant's name, your colors. Climbo is invisible.

tags: [features, white-label, branding] sources: [BRocA7husPU, PQiNrTKVLOU, YOUTUBE-ANALYSIS] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
White-label is the business model that makes Climbo worth €99/month. You are not reselling "Climbo." You are selling your own AI reputation management platform. Your clients see your brand everywhere: the login page, the dashboard, the email notifications, the AI assistant name, the review link they share with customers.

From Climbo's 2026 video "Your AI, Fully Branded":

"Give your AI its own name, logo, and identity. You can even personalize every single agent — SEO, Social, and GEO."

docs.title: How it works
docs.text: All white-label settings live in Brand in your agency dashboard at my.climbo.com.

docs.title: What you can customize
docs.text: Element What to set Where clients see it Brand Name Your agency name Login page, emails, dashboard header Custom Domain e.g., app.youragency.com Browser URL bar, every link Logo Your logo file Login page, dashboard, emails Colors Primary/secondary colors Buttons, highlights, accents Font Custom web font All text in the platform AI Name e.g., "Aria", "MaxAI" Chatbot, agent activity, email subject lines AI Logo Custom avatar for your AI Chatbot interface "Powered By" URL Your domain Footer of the platform Free Trial Banner Custom upgrade message Clients on trial plan Custom Code JS/CSS injections Platform-wide customization

docs.title: Custom Domain Setup
docs.text:
1. Purchase a subdomain for your agency (e.g., app.youragency.com)
  2. In Climbo Brand settings, enter this domain
  3. Add a CNAME DNS record pointing to Climbo's servers (Climbo provides the target address)
  4. Wait 5–30 minutes for DNS propagation
  5. Your clients now access the platform at your domain — no reference to Climbo in the URL

docs.title: AI Agent Personalization
docs.text: Each of the three AI agents can be individually branded. In Brand settings: - Set a prompt sentence that defines the AI's personality and approach - Customize the agent names (what clients see when they look at agent activity) - Add a custom AI logo that appears in the chat interface

docs.title: AI Chatbot
docs.text:
Enable the AI Chatbot in Brand settings. When active, your clients interact with your named AI assistant via chat instead of navigating the dashboard manually. The chatbot can: - Send review request campaigns - Update GBP info - Add contacts - Manage templates

This is especially useful for tech-averse clients. They don't learn a dashboard; they talk to an AI assistant that has your name and branding.

docs.title: Email branding
docs.text:
Every automated email your clients receive (welcome email, review notifications, billing reminders) comes from your agency's SMTP if configured. See [[operations/smtp-setup]] for setup details.

Without custom SMTP, emails still show your agency name but may come from a Climbo sending domain.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• Test your branded platform before the first client call. Open a private/incognito browser window and visit your custom domain. See exactly what your client will see. Fix anything that looks off.
  • The login page is a sales asset. It is the first thing a prospect sees when you show them "the platform." Make sure it looks professional.
  • White-label extends to the review link. The link you share with your client's customers shows your client's branding — their logo, their business name. Climbo's name never appears.
  • Support access. Clients cannot see the Support link that leads to Climbo. They see only your agency's contact methods, which you configure.

docs.title: Account Setup
docs.text:
One-line summary: Everything you need to configure before signing your first client — brand, billing, email, and balance.

tags: [getting-started, setup] sources: [BRocA7husPU, PLAYBOOK] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
When you first log into my.climbo.com, you land on a mostly-blank agency dashboard. Before you touch the client section, complete your agency setup. This takes 1–2 hours and you only do it once.

The dashboard has six main sections: Clients, Plans, Brand, Sales, Settings, Support.

docs.title: Step 1 — Configure your Brand
docs.text:
Go to Brand in the left sidebar. This is where Climbo becomes invisible and your brand takes over.

Set: - Brand Name — your agency name (what clients see everywhere) - Custom Domain — a subdomain like app.youragency.com (point it to Climbo's DNS) - Logo — your agency logo, shown on the login page and inside the platform - Colors and Font — match your existing brand - AI Brand Name — the name your AI assistant shows to clients (e.g., "Aria" or "MaxAI") - Powered By — set to your domain so emails and footers link back to you

Also in Brand, you can configure: - SEO Agent settings (default tone, language, keywords) - Social Agent settings - GEO Agent settings - Free Trial Banner — shown to trial clients to prompt upgrade - AI Chatbot — enables a chat widget your clients use instead of the dashboard

docs.title: Step 2 — Set up Stripe billing
docs.text:
Go to Settings → Finance to connect Stripe. Stripe handles all subscription billing between you and your clients.

Once connected, you can create pricing plans, accept credit cards and SEPA direct debit, and view your MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue) in the Sales dashboard.

Note for EU operators: Stripe does not emit Italian-format electronic invoices (SDI). If you operate in Italy or another EU country with specific invoicing requirements, integrate a tool like Fatture in Cloud before your first paying client.

docs.title: Step 3 — Create your Plans
docs.text:
Go to Plans and create at least one subscription plan. Example: - Name: "Starter Reputation" - Price: $149/month - Features: review collection, SEO Agent, GBP management

You can create multiple plans (basic, pro, premium) and control which features each plan includes.

docs.title: Step 4 — Configure SMTP
docs.text: Go to Brand → SMTP or Settings to configure a custom email sender. Without this, review request emails go out from a default Climbo domain. With SMTP configured, they come from hello@youragency.com — fully branded.

docs.title: Step 5 — Set up Balance
docs.text:
Go to Settings → Balance and add credit. Balance is the pre-paid pool that covers SMS, WhatsApp, and AI token costs.

Cost reference: | Channel | Approx. cost | |---------|-------------| | SMS (EU) | ~€0.05–0.10 per message | | WhatsApp | ~$0.03 per message | | Email | Free | | AI Tokens | $6.60 per 1M tokens |

Add at least $20–50 before activating your first client on SMS or WhatsApp.

docs.title: Step 6 — Customize Email Templates
docs.text: Go to Settings → Emails / Templates to edit the Welcome Email your clients receive when they join. Make the tone match your agency — warm, direct, and free of any technical jargon. This is the first thing they read after signing up.

docs.title: Step 7 — Test with the Demo Client
docs.text: Climbo provides a demo client account. Walk through the full client experience before you bring in real clients: - Connect a Google account - Set up a review link - Enable the SEO Agent - Send a test review request

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• Your custom domain needs a DNS CNAME record pointing to Climbo's server. Contact support if you need help — it typically takes 5–10 minutes once the record propagates.
  • The agency dashboard and the client dashboard are different. You see everything; clients see only their own data.
  • Coupons (Settings → Finance → Coupons) let you give clients a discounted or free trial period — useful for your first wave of customers.

docs.title: First Client Checklist
docs.text:
One-line summary: The exact sequence to take a new client from signed to flywheel-active in 24 hours.

tags: [getting-started, operations, onboarding] sources: [BRocA7husPU, PLAYBOOK, skool-this-business-is-passive] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Onboarding is not optional. The Skool community says it plainly:

"Onboarding is not optional — it's a must. The better you get your clients set up, the longer they stay and the better they pay." — Community post, 26 comments

A client you sign but don't properly onboard is a churn risk within 30 days. A client who sees results in week one stays for years.

docs.title: Before the client's first call (Day 0)
docs.text:
• [ ] Create the client in your Climbo dashboard (Clients → Add Client)
  • [ ] Assign the correct plan
  • [ ] Apply any coupon code if they're on a trial
  • [ ] Send the Welcome Email (customize the template first — see [[operations/onboarding-clients]])
  • [ ] Book the onboarding call with a specific time. No vague "we'll connect soon." An actual calendar invite.

docs.title: Onboarding call (Day 1–2) — record it
docs.text:
This call does everything. It typically takes 45–60 minutes.

Google Business Profile connection: - [ ] Client shares GBP access (you request Manager access via Google, or they do it with you on screen) - [ ] Connect GBP to Climbo (Settings → Integrations → Google) - [ ] Verify all GBP information is accurate: hours, phone, address, services, photos

Review collection setup — pick the right method:

Business type Best method Restaurant, salon, gym QR code kiosk + NFC card at reception Professional services (lawyer, dentist) Manual quick request via WhatsApp/SMS after each appointment Business with a CRM Auto-send via Zapier/Google Sheets integration
  • [ ] Generate and print the QR code page (Climbo generates it automatically — print and laminate)
  • [ ] Order/configure the NFC card if using one (see [[features/nfc-cards]])
  • [ ] Create at least one review request template (SMS, WhatsApp, or Email)
  • [ ] Test the review link: open it on your phone, confirm it shows the client's branding

AI Agents: - [ ] Enable SEO Agent — set language, keywords (business name + city + main service) - [ ] Enable Social Agent — connect Facebook page and/or Instagram - [ ] Enable GEO Agent if on a plan that includes it - [ ] Run "reply to all past reviews" if the client has existing unanswered reviews

Establish the baseline: - [ ] Screenshot current Google review count and rating ("before" number) - [ ] Note the date. This is Day 1 of your results tracking.

docs.title: Day 7 — proactive touch
docs.text:
• [ ] Take a screenshot of any new reviews that have come in
  • [ ] Send it to the client via WhatsApp without being asked
  • This takes 2 minutes. It is worth its weight in trust.

docs.title: Day 14 — check-in call
docs.text:
• [ ] Show the before/after review count
  • [ ] Adjust anything not working (review collection volume too low? Change the trigger timing)
  • [ ] Ask: "Who else do you know in a similar situation?"

docs.title: Day 90 — harvest
docs.text:
• [ ] Review count should have grown visibly
  • [ ] Ask for referrals and any testimonials about working with you

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• The review collection process is 90% of the results. If your client is not collecting reviews, nothing else matters. Verify it is working live on the call, not later.
  • Train the client's staff to ask for the scan/review in person. The QR code sitting on the counter does nothing if employees don't mention it.
  • Never promise specific review counts or ratings. Promise a working system.
  • If the client is not tech-savvy, use the Climbo AI Chatbot option — they manage everything through a chat interface instead of a dashboard.

docs.title: Pricing and Plans
docs.text:
One-line summary: You pay €99/month to Climbo; you charge clients whatever you want — typical range is $99–$500/month.

tags: [getting-started, pricing, business-model] sources: [BRocA7husPU, PQiNrTKVLOU, PLAYBOOK] last_updated: 2026-07-11

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Climbo's pricing to you is simple: €99/month flat. Unlimited clients. All features. No per-seat fees, no percentage of revenue.

Your pricing to clients is entirely up to you. You set the plans, the names, the prices, and what features each plan includes. Climbo's Plans section is where you create these.

docs.title: What you pay Climbo
docs.text:
• €99/month agency fee (billed to your card)
  • Plus Balance top-ups for SMS/WhatsApp/AI token usage (see [[features/balance-explained]])
  • No setup fees. No per-client fees.

docs.title: What you charge clients
docs.text:
Create plans in your Climbo dashboard under Plans. Common structures in the community:

Single-tier (simple start): - One plan at $99–$149/month - Includes: review collection, SEO Agent, GBP management - Easiest to sell, easiest to deliver

Three-tier (scales revenue per client): | Tier | Price | What's included | |------|-------|----------------| | Starter | $99/mo | Review collection + SEO Agent | | Growth | $249/mo | + Social Agent + GBP posts | | Authority | $499/mo | + GEO Agent + AI search optimization |

Community member wins at various price points: - "Picked up a new flooring contractor — $299/month" (Skool, 19 comments) - "1 x Beauty salon $1,800 annual" (Skool) - "5 restaurants on annual plan — 3,000€ in one week" (Skool, 28 comments)

docs.title: The math to $3k/month
docs.text:
From Giacomo's training: - 30 face-to-face outreach per month - ~30% say yes to free trial (10 free trials/month) - ~30% of trials convert to paid ($100/month) - = 3 new paying clients per month - After 12 months: 36 paying clients × $100 = $3,600 MRR

These are conservative numbers. Multiple community members have surpassed this.

docs.title: Free trials
docs.text:
Climbo has a built-in free trial mechanism. You can give a client free access for 7–14 days (configurable). During the trial, they collect real reviews and see the system work. Once they see reviews coming in, conversion is almost guaranteed.

Use coupons (Settings → Finance → Coupons) to: - Offer 100% discount for a defined period (free trial) - Give a fixed discount for early adopters - Create time-limited promotions

docs.title: Annual plans
docs.text: Offer annual pricing at a discount (e.g., 2 months free = 10× monthly price). Benefits: - You get cash upfront - Client is committed for 12 months - Zero churn risk for a year - Multiple community members have sold annual plans successfully

docs.title: Stripe integration
docs.text: Plans you create in Climbo connect directly to Stripe for automated billing. Clients enter their card once; billing is automatic forever. You do not chase invoices. See [[features/stripe-billing]] for setup details.

docs.title: Key details / Tips
docs.text:
• Start simple. One plan at $100–$149/month is easier to close than explaining three tiers to a first prospect.
  • Annual upfront is your best retention tool. A client who paid for a year never churns in month 3.
  • Balance is separate from your plan fee. If you run SMS campaigns, make sure you have enough balance. Email is free.
  • Don't undercharge. Community feedback is clear: charging $99/month gets the same resistance as $149/month, but $149 gives you double the cushion.
